target.name: Orangewood Labs
target.founder: Abhinav Das, Aditya Bhatia, Akash Bansal

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $105M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Orangewood Labs is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Abhinav Das, Aditya Bhatia, and Akash Bansal. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of nine companies (one public, eight private) operating in the same sector and comparable or adjacent stages, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the company's $5M+ total raised as a proxy base value of $105M and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $210M. The peer-median method examines six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.1B, though the peer range extends from $300M to $10.0B, reflecting wide dispersion within the frontier robotics market. 1X Technologies at $10.0B represents a significant upside outlier; Fairmat and Collaborative Robotics anchor the lower end at $350M and $500M respectively.

The triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $178M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $642M. The wide range reflects genuine uncertainty: peer valuations in frontier robotics exhibit substantial dispersion, and the stage-progression method produces a meaningfully lower output than the peer median, likely because the peer set skews toward later-stage companies. Key uncertainties center on Orangewood's relative competitive position within the cohort and the sustainability of valuation multiples in this nascent sector.
